title: Author ShowcaseShellView and the app's root widget chain
description: Build the visible shell of the Turbo Widgets Shop app — the stateless view that renders the current showcase screen inside the package's own contextual navigation chrome. Also finalise the root widget chain in main.dart so the app boots into a fully-wrapped ShadApp → ContextualButtonsProvider → TResponsiveBuilder → ShowcaseShellView tree.

context.view_pattern: Every view in this app is stateless and delegates all state to its view model via TViewBuilder from turbo_widgets. The shell view is no exception: it is a stateless TViewBuilder<ShowcaseShellViewModel> that reads reactive cells via ValueListenableBuilder / MultiListenableBuilder from turbo_notifiers.
context.chrome_composition: The top chrome is a TContextualAppBar showing the current area title, a back button when on a product detail, and a sun/moon theme toggle action. The nav chrome is a TContextualBottomNavigation on mobile and a TContextualSideNavigation on desktop, holding one tab per aisle plus a 'Shop' home shortcut on the desktop side rail. Device type is decided by TResponsiveBuilder at the root.
context.contextual_buttons_integration: The shell view hosts TContextualButtons by setting TViewBuilder.hostContextualButtons=true. Each showcase view later pushes its own TContextualButtonsConfig through the ContextualButtonsProvider lookup — the shell just hosts the overlay, it does not push config.
context.inner_content: The body of the shell renders the currently-active showcase view by resolving the current route through ShowcaseRouter (a placeholder router in phase 2 — real routes get wired in the final integration task).

goals: example/lib/shell/views/showcase_shell/showcase_shell_view.dart contains ShowcaseShellView as a StatelessWidget wrapping a TViewBuilder<ShowcaseShellViewModel> with hostContextualButtons=true.
example/lib/main.dart runs the app through a root widget chain: ShadApp.material(theme from ThemeService) → ContextualButtonsProvider<ShowcaseRoute> → TResponsiveBuilder → ShowcaseShellView.

The top app bar shows the correct title per route (Shop on home, {Aisle Label} on aisle detail, {Widget Name} on product detail), a back button on detail routes only, and a theme-toggle action that calls the view model's theme toggle method.
The bottom/side nav renders one button per aisle plus (on desktop side rail) a Shop home shortcut. Selecting a button routes to that aisle via the view model.
Resizing the window at runtime correctly swaps between the bottom nav and side rail via TResponsiveBuilder.
Theme toggle visibly flips the entire app between ShadThemeData light and dark on the platform you are testing.
non_goals: Implementing any showcase screen's body — routes still resolve to placeholders (those are replaced in phases 4 and 5).
Pushing per-view TContextualButtonsConfig entries — the final integration task wires those.
Owning any mutable state — all state lives on the view model.
acceptance_criteria: `flutter run` launches the example app into a functional shell that displays shop home's placeholder and all nav chrome.
Theme toggle visibly swaps light/dark across the whole app.
Resizing the window from phone width to tablet width swaps the nav between bottom bar and side rail with no glitches.
Tapping an aisle button in the nav updates the shell's current route.
Tapping the back button on a product route returns to the originating aisle detail.
No Material widgets (Scaffold, AppBar, Drawer, BottomNavigationBar) are used inside the shell — only ShadApp + turbo_widgets chrome.
constraints: Follow dev-conventions/views-and-view-models.md — the view is stateless; all state flows from the view model through ValueListenable.
Follow dev-conventions/folders-and-file-types.md — snake_case filenames, one type per file.
No Material AppBar, Drawer, BottomNavigationBar — only the package's own contextual chrome widgets.
Icons come exclusively from LucideIcons bundled with shadcn_ui.
Do not duplicate chrome layout between mobile and desktop — let TResponsiveBuilder swap the nav placement and share the rest.
tools_to_use: RepoPrompt MCP (read_file) to understand TContextualAppBar, TContextualBottomNavigation, TContextualSideNavigation, TContextualButtons, TViewBuilder, TResponsiveBuilder APIs post-refactor.
dart-mcp (launch_app, hot_reload, get_widget_tree) for interactive iteration.
Bash for `flutter run` on your chosen platform.

testing_and_verification.unit_tests: None — this is a pure UI task and the view is stateless. UI-level verification is manual visual inspection per the spec's explicit non-goal of tests for the example.
verification_gate: Manual launch + visual verification of every acceptance criterion: theme toggle, nav swap on resize, back button, aisle routing.
Use dart-mcp get_widget_tree to confirm ShadApp and TContextualButtons are actually wrapping the tree, not Material ancestors.

quality_checklist.maintainability: Adding a new aisle requires adding an enum value + catalog entry — the shell view does not need a code change.
quality_checklist.dry: No chrome layout duplication across mobile/desktop branches.
quality_checklist.single_responsibility: The shell view renders chrome and delegates inner content — nothing else.
quality_checklist.performance: ValueListenableBuilder scopes are tight so chrome updates do not rebuild the inner showcase view.

workflow_instructions: Read TContextualAppBar, TContextualBottomNavigation, TContextualSideNavigation, and TViewBuilder source before typing a line — their APIs dictate the wiring.
Add `follow_up_notes` to 19-wire-routing-and-contextual-buttons-integration-task with any gotchas about ContextualButtonsProvider registration the integration task should know about.
follow_up_notes: Phase 3 task 03 created the ShowcaseShellViewModel. Public API:
State (ValueListenable getters): currentRoute (ShowcaseRoute), selectedAisleKey (String?), selectedProductKey (String?), brightness (Brightness — delegated from ThemeService)
Mutators: navigateToShopHome(), navigateToAisle(String aisleKey), openProduct(ShowcaseRoute route), goBack(), toggleTheme()
DI: registered via ShowcaseShellViewModel.registerFactory() in LocatorService._registerFactories()
goBack pops: product→aisle (preserves aisleKey), aisle→home, home→no-op
